neighbors.claims: Josue and Diana Flores are associated with a parcel at 375 W Buffalo Ln in Huachuca City, which is also the registered address for "J & D Roll Off & Hauling" ([FMCSA](https://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov/query.asp?searchtype=ANY&query_type=queryCarrierSnapshot&query_param=USDOT&query_string=2345532)). The property is classified as industrial or commercial land in some listings ([LoopNet](https://www.loopnet.com/property/375-w-buffalo-ln-huachuca-city-az-85616/04003-10606019A/)). No public meeting minutes or news articles were found that mention the couple’s views on area development or community issues.
neighbors.influence_justification: Local business owners (J & D Roll Off)

approach_recommendations.engage: The Flores family appears to operate a local hauling business from the property. Approach with a focus on how project construction might interact with their business operations and road access.

neighbors.name: Loren J. Sheldon
neighbors.claims: Loren J. Sheldon is the operator of the Whetstone Pit, a surface metal mine specializing in sand and gravel ([MinesDatabase](https://minesdatabase.com/mines/view/203001)). He has also operated L. Sheldon & Trucking Company, a heavy equipment business, for over 20 years ([Arizona Daily Independent](https://arizonadailyindependent.com/2021/03/07/four-federal-agents-accused-in-lawsuit-of-fabricating-evidence-and-false-statements/)). Sheldon has been involved in litigation against federal agents regarding investigations into his business operations, which were ultimately dismissed ([Arizona Daily Independent](https://arizonadailyindependent.com/2021/03/07/four-federal-agents-accused-in-lawsuit-of-fabricating-evidence-and-false-statements/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Longtime business owner (Mining/Trucking)

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage Sheldon via a technical liaison who can address infrastructure and drainage plans. His history as a heavy equipment operator suggests he will have specific technical concerns regarding road use and site grading.

neighbors.name: John & Gail Christmann
neighbors.claims: John and Gail Christmann moved to the Sonoita area from New Paltz, New York, in 2020 ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/PRT-5.23-for-web.pdf)). Gail Christmann is highly active in local planning and preservation efforts; she serves on the Sonoita/Elgin Preservation and Planning Committee, which formed to give the community control over future growth ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/planning-committee-sets-april-29-town-hall-in-sonoita/)). She leads the local Dark Sky certification initiative, advocating for lighting that minimizes pollution to benefit wildlife ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/citizen-committee-maps-new-planning-preservation-priorities-for-sonoita-elgin-area/)). Additionally, she is involved in revitalizing the Sonoita-Elgin Chamber of Commerce ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/revitalization-efforts-underway-for-sonoita-elgin-chamber-of-commerce/)). Gail has identified herself as a "rancher and cattlewoman" in a letter to the editor defending the beef industry ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/letters-to-the-editor-4/)). Prior to moving, Gail served as a member of the Zoning Board of Appeals in the Town of New Paltz, NY ([townofnewpaltzny.gov](https://www.townofnewpaltzny.gov/zoning-board/minutes/minutes-17)). John Christmann is the founder of StarCrest Inspections, a home inspection business serving Cochise County ([starcresthomeinspections.com](https://starcresthomeinspections.com/areas/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Gail is a Preservation Committee member, Chamber organizer, and Dark Sky leader.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage Gail as a knowledgeable stakeholder with zoning experience. Frame the project around responsible planning, preservation of rural character, and Dark Sky compliance to align with her established priorities.

citations.claim: John and Gail Christmann moved to the Sonoita area from New Paltz, New York, in 2020
citations.title: Patagonia Regional Times May 2023

citations.title: Revitalization Efforts Underway for Chamber

citations.claim: identified herself as a 'rancher and cattlewoman'
citations.title: Letters to the Editor - PRT

citations.claim: served as a member of the Zoning Board of Appeals in the Town of New Paltz, NY

neighbors.verification_notes: Draft profile significantly understated influence. Gail Christmann is a key community organizer in Sonoita/Elgin (neighboring Cochise) with specific focus on planning and preservation. Stance on renewables is unknown, but she is active in 'controlled growth' advocacy.

neighbors.name: Darrel D Howell & Lee Ann Howell
neighbors.claims: Darrel D. Howell and Lee Ann Howell are residents of Cochise County, Arizona. Lee Ann Howell is a prominent science teacher at Colonel Smith Middle School in the Fort Huachuca Accommodation School District ([source](https://www.schools.pima.gov/media/press-releases/educators-from-southern-arizona-selected-for-the-stemazing-teacher-leader-p)). She holds significant community standing as the 2018 Cochise County Teacher of the Year and a 2019 Arizona Teacher of the Year Ambassador for Excellence ([source](https://cochiseeducationfoundation.org/recognition/)) ([source](https://events.nau.edu/event/tariat/)). Darrel Howell is a federal employee with the USDA Forest Service, serving as a fire and fuels management contact for the Nogales Ranger District of the Coronado National Forest, which manages lands across Cochise, Pima, and Santa Cruz counties ([source](https://www.fs.usda.gov/sopa/components/reports/sopa-110305-2024-01.pdf)). Lee Ann Howell is documented attending meetings for the Sulphur Springs Valley Electric Cooperative (SSVEC) in Willcox, indicating engagement with local utility matters ([source](https://www.ssvec.org/downloads/minutes/Minutes-2016-07.pdf)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Award-winning educator and USDA Forest Service official

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage regarding environmental stewardship given Darrel's Forest Service role. Highlight tax revenue benefits for local schools, appealing to Lee Ann's position as a celebrated educator.

neighbors.name: Babacomari Ranch Company
neighbors.claims: Babacomari Ranch Company is a family-owned cattle ranch (~28,000 acres) spanning from Sonoita into Cochise County, originally acquired in 1935 by Frank Cullen Brophy; today it is held by approximately 65 Brophy descendants and is Arizona’s largest contiguous private land parcel [Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/county-to-consider-permit-for-new-40-60-million-dude-ranch-in-sonoita/) [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonoita_AVA). In January 2020, the owners partnered with Arizona Land and Water Trust to place 2,488 acres of the ranch under a permanent conservation easement, protecting habitat and the working landscape [Arizona Land and Water Trust](https://alwt.org/babacomari-ranch/) [Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/elgin-ranch-works-to-preserve-watershed/). The family has also pursued new development on the property: in September 2024, they received a Conditional Use Permit to develop a 150-guest "dude ranch" resort on a 2,200-acre pasture in Sonoita, an upscale project estimated to cost $40–60 million [Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/new-40-60-million-dude-ranch-gets-first-permit/) [Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/PRT-10.24-for-web.pdf). The ranch utilizes solar technology for operations, including solar-powered fencing and pumps [Arizona Water Protection Fund](https://www.azwpf.gov/sites/default/files/media/09-164FinalReportBabocomariRiverRiparianProtectioneditedon1-24-14ljk.pdf) [Arizona Land and Water Trust](https://alwt.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/ALWT-AR-2024-ToPress_Amended-website.pdf).
neighbors.influence_justification: Controls vast acreage (28,000 acres); Historic family legacy since 1935; Active in conservation and capable of large-scale development projects

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage the Brophy family’s representatives (e.g., their project advisor) early; emphasize alignment with the ranch’s conservation goals and address any wildlife or scenery impacts in the proposal

citations.claim: Babacomari Ranch Company is a family-owned cattle ranch (~28,000 acres)... originally acquired in 1935 by Frank Cullen Brophy
citations.title: County to consider permit for new $40-60 million dude ranch in Sonoita

citations.claim: Arizona’s largest contiguous private land parcel
citations.title: Sonoita AVA - Wikipedia

citations.title: New $40-60 million dude ranch gets first permit

citations.claim: The ranch utilizes solar technology for operations, including solar-powered fencing and pumps

neighbors.verification_notes: The family supports conservation and selective commercial development (dude ranch) but has opposed housing subdivisions. They utilize solar technology for ranch operations.

neighbors.name: Mountain View Rv Resort Llc
neighbors.claims: Mountain View RV Resort LLC is a privately held company that acquired the RV campground at 99 W Vista Lane in Huachuca City in March 2019 [Real Estate Daily News](https://realestatedaily-news.com/park-brokerage-closes-on-rv-park-mhp-in-arizona-california/?print=print). The park spans approximately 15 acres and contains 81 spaces, marketing the area’s open desert skies and mountain views between the Huachuca and Mule Mountains as key attractions [Real Estate Daily News](https://realestatedaily-news.com/park-brokerage-closes-on-rv-park-mhp-in-arizona-california/?print=print) [Mountain View RV Park](https://mountainviewrvpark.com/). The entity is registered in Arizona [Mountain View RV Park](https://mountainviewrvpark.com/terms-and-conditions/).
neighbors.influence_justification: Local commercial enterprise (15-acre RV park); serves transient snowbird population

approach_recommendations.engage: Reach out privately to the RV park owner; offer measures (landscaping, traffic management) to mitigate any impacts on the park’s ambiance and visibility

The trust has also been associated with property transactions in Maricopa County, AZ [City of Scottsdale](https://eservices.scottsdaleaz.gov/bldgresources/EDM/Viewer?docId=13129392&docName=CITIZEN%20REVIEW%20%2817382435-v1%29&fileExt=pdf).

neighbors.name: Decker Roy F Living Trust
neighbors.claims: The Roy F Decker Living Trust was the former owner of a residential property at 5520 W Desert Mountain Trail in Bisbee (Cochise County), which was sold to private individuals in April 2022 [Homes.com](https://www.homes.com/property/5520-w-desert-mountain-trail-bisbee-az/ej2y0n2f4945j/). Roy F. Decker (individual) is deceased [Ancestry](https://www.ancestry.com/search/categories/bmd_death/?name=Roy_Decker&event=_OR).
neighbors.influence_justification: Trust appears to have divested local property in 2022; current status as a neighbor is doubtful unless other parcels are owned.

neighbors.verification_notes: The trust sold the identified property in 2022. It is unclear if they still own the specific parcel associated with the PIN provided in the input, but public records link the trust to the 2022 sale.

neighbors.name: R V City Inc
neighbors.claims: RV City, Inc. is a recreational vehicle dealership incorporated in Arizona on August 3, 1987 ([BBB](https://www.bbb.org/us/az/huachuca-city/profile/rv-dealers/rv-city-inc-1286-16902)). While the business was long operated by President Ronald Hoffman, the company announced it is under new ownership and management as of May 2025 ([RV City](https://www.rvcity.net/about-us)). The company has a history of federal contracting, including supplying travel trailers to FEMA for disaster relief in 2005 ([FEMA](https://disability.law.uiowa.edu/dpn_hi/100.pdf)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Long-established local business (since 1987) with history of federal contracts; recently underwent ownership change.

approach_recommendations.engage: Contact the new management team (post-May 2025 ownership change) to introduce the project. Acknowledge the business's long history in the community and ensure construction logistics do not interfere with dealership access or operations.

neighbors.name: Nye James W Revocable Living Trust Agreement
neighbors.claims: Registered owner of property in Cochise County. Historical records indicate a James W. Nye served as a Superior Court Judge in Cochise County and died in 1958 ([Phoenix Masonry](https://www.phoenixmasonry.org/10,000_famous_freemasons/volume_4_q_to_z.htm)), though a direct link between the historical figure and the current trust beneficiaries could not be definitively verified.
neighbors.influence_justification: Private landowner with no identified current public profile or recent political activity.

approach_recommendations.engage: Standard neighbor outreach letter to registered address.

neighbors.name: Bouchikas Family Revocable Lifetime Trust
neighbors.claims: Property is associated with the Bouchikas family. Chris Bouchikas has been active in the local astronomy community and has previously met with Cochise County officials to advocate for light pollution controls ([HAC](https://hacastro.groups.io/g/main/topics?page=878&after=1023676515000000000)). The parcel is classified for mobile/manufactured home use ([LoopNet](https://www.loopnet.com/property/2098-n-cougar-pl-douglas-az-85607/04003-10606026C/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Private landowner with documented history of engaging county officials on specific local issues (light pollution).

approach_recommendations.engage: Address potential concerns regarding night lighting during construction or operation. Emphasize 'dark sky compliant' lighting in project design to align with the family's demonstrated interest in astronomy.

overview_summary: Of the 25 neighbors profiled, 19 are individuals and 6 are organizations, with 24 maintaining an unknown stance and one identified as neutral. High influence is held by two entities—the Babacomari Ranch Company, a 28,000-acre conservation-focused cattle ranch, and local business owner Loren Sheldon—while six neighbors hold medium influence, including RV-related businesses and a law enforcement professional. Although no formal opposition exists, the ranch’s stewardship priorities and the RV park’s tourism interests represent potential risks if development affects land-use values. The remaining 17 neighbors are primarily private family trusts with low public profiles and limited community influence.
